title: Coping with Peak Cypress and Willow Pollen Across Cities
summary: Spring pollen from cypress and willow trees causes allergies in Chinese cities, prompting both individual protective measures and urban solutions.

levels.title: Spring Pollen Causes Allergies
levels.content:
Spring has arrived in many Chinese cities. The weather is warm and trees are growing. But many people feel uncomfortable. They sneeze and their eyes feel itchy. Why? The answer is pollen.

Pollen comes from trees like cypress and willow. People cannot see it, but it is in the air. When people breathe it, they may get allergies. Young and middle-aged people often have this problem because they spend more time outside.

What can people do? They can wear masks and stay inside when pollen is high. They should also sleep well and eat healthy food. Cities are helping too. Beijing uses special spray to reduce pollen. Some cities are also planting different trees that make less pollen.

vocabulary.explanation: tiny powder from plants that can cause allergies
to suddenly push air out through your nose
having an uncomfortable feeling that makes you want to scratch

quiz.question: Why do many people feel uncomfortable in spring?
options: Because the weather is too cold
Because pollen in the air causes allergies

levels.title: Cities Battle Spring Pollen Allergies
levels.content:
As spring arrives in Chinese cities, a familiar problem returns: pollen allergies. While the warm weather and budding trees signal the season's arrival, many residents experience sneezing, itchy eyes, and nasal discomfort. The main culprits are cypress and willow trees, which release large amounts of pollen into the air.

According to health experts, pollen allergies are essentially an overreaction of the immune system to harmless substances. Young and middle-aged adults are particularly affected because their immune systems are more reactive and they spend more time outdoors. The symptoms can last for weeks during peak pollen season, typically from late March to early April.

Experts recommend several protective measures. People should avoid outdoor activities when pollen levels are high, wear masks, and use air purifiers indoors. Maintaining good sleep, a balanced diet, and regular exercise can also strengthen immunity and reduce allergy risks.

Cities are taking action too. Beijing has developed a pollen fixation agent that helps pollen settle faster, proving 17 to 20 times more effective than traditional water spraying. Additionally, urban planners are replacing high-pollen male cypress trees with female varieties that don't produce airborne pollen.25:T789,As spring unfolds across Chinese cities, the annual pollen season brings more than just blooming trees and warmer weather. For a significant portion of urban residents, it marks the onset of allergic symptomsГўВЂВ”sneezing, itchy eyes, and nasal congestionГўВЂВ”triggered primarily by cypress and willow pollen. According to the China Meteorological Administration, cypress pollen has dominated since March, with willow pollen spreading across North China.

Unlike visible air pollution, pollen operates as an almost invisible seasonal health concern. Zhao Xiyao, a postgraduate student in Beijing, describes the experience: "In the morning and evening, it feels like there's a fine layer of dust in the air. As soon as I step outside, I start sneezing." Her coping strategyГўВЂВ”temporary relief through eye drops and nasal sprayГўВЂВ”reflects a common "endure and wait" mentality among sufferers.

Medical experts explain that allergic reactions represent an excessive immune response to typically harmless substances. Young and middle-aged adults face heightened vulnerability due to more reactive immune systems and greater outdoor exposure. Luo Chaofan, a rehabilitation therapist, emphasizes that sustainable coping mechanismsГўВЂВ”including adequate sleep, balanced nutrition, and regular exerciseГўВЂВ”prove more effective than temporary fixes.

Meanwhile, urban governance is evolving to incorporate pollen management. Beijing has introduced a pollen fixation agent that accelerates particle settling with 17 to 20 times greater efficiency than conventional water spraying, requiring application only once daily or every two days. Long-term strategies include replacing high-allergen male cypress trees with female varieties that produce no airborne pollen, thereby maintaining aesthetic greenery while reducing public health risks. Young and middle-aged people often have this problem because they spend more time outside.\n\nWhat can people do? They can wear masks and stay inside when pollen is high. They should also sleep well and eat healthy food. Cities are helping too. Beijing uses special spray to reduce pollen. vocabulary.explanation: things or people responsible for causing a problem
a response that is too strong or extreme
the body's ability to resist disease and infection
the process of making something stay in one place

quiz.question: Why are young and middle-aged adults more affected by pollen allergies?
options: Because they have weaker immune systems than children
Because their immune systems are more reactive and they are outdoors more often
Because they don't use air purifiers at home

quiz.question: What long-term solution are cities implementing to reduce pollen?
options: Cutting down all cypress and willow trees
Replacing male cypress trees with female varieties that produce no airborne pollen
Spraying water on trees five times every day

levels.title: Urban Strategies Emerge to Combat Seasonal Pollen Allergies
levels.content:
As spring unfolds across Chinese cities, the annual pollen season brings more than just blooming trees and warmer weather. For a significant portion of urban residents, it marks the onset of allergic symptomsГўВЂВ”sneezing, itchy eyes, and nasal congestionГўВЂВ”triggered primarily by cypress and willow pollen. According to the China Meteorological Administration, cypress pollen has dominated since March, with willow pollen spreading across North China.

Unlike visible air pollution, pollen operates as an almost invisible seasonal health concern. Zhao Xiyao, a postgraduate student in Beijing, describes the experience: "In the morning and evening, it feels like there's a fine layer of dust in the air. As soon as I step outside, I start sneezing." Her coping strategyГўВЂВ”temporary relief through eye drops and nasal sprayГўВЂВ”reflects a common "endure and wait" mentality among sufferers.

Medical experts explain that allergic reactions represent an excessive immune response to typically harmless substances. Young and middle-aged adults face heightened vulnerability due to more reactive immune systems and greater outdoor exposure. Luo Chaofan, a rehabilitation therapist, emphasizes that sustainable coping mechanismsГўВЂВ”including adequate sleep, balanced nutrition, and regular exerciseГўВЂВ”prove more effective than temporary fixes.

Meanwhile, urban governance is evolving to incorporate pollen management. Beijing has introduced a pollen fixation agent that accelerates particle settling with 17 to 20 times greater efficiency than conventional water spraying, requiring application only once daily or every two days. Long-term strategies include replacing high-allergen male cypress trees with female varieties that produce no airborne pollen, thereby maintaining aesthetic greenery while reducing public health risks. vocabulary.explanation: the beginning or start of something, especially something unpleasant
a blocked or crowded condition, especially in the nose or airways
the state of being exposed to harm or risk
the system or method of managing and controlling an organization or area
deliberate action taken to improve a situation or prevent it from getting worse

quiz.question: What distinguishes pollen from industrial pollution according to the article?
options: Pollen is almost invisible yet accumulates as a seasonal health concern
Pollen only affects people with chronic conditions
Pollen can be completely eliminated through water spraying

quiz.question: Why does Luo Chaofan recommend sustainable lifestyle habits over temporary fixes?
options: Because pollen allergies are recurrent and seasonal, lasting weeks or longer
Because eye drops and nasal sprays are too expensive for most people
Because outdoor activities should be completely avoided during spring

quiz.question: What advantage does the pollen fixation agent offer compared to traditional methods?
options: It completely eliminates all pollen from the environment permanently
It is 17 to 20 times more efficient and requires less frequent application
It changes male cypress trees into female trees naturally
